title: stisseria reclinata
intro: Stisseria reclinata, also known as the climbing milkwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae. It is a perennial climber found in tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Stisseria reclinata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
